name: Documentation

tasks.title: Update human documentation for knowledge files
tasks.description: Update human documentation for knowledge files
tasks.body:
Update the human-facing documentation to cover codebase knowledge files:

1. docs/humans/how-it-works.md: Add a section explaining codebase knowledge files, how they fit into the execution context alongside ADRs, specs, and AARs. Explain the lifecycle: agents discover something non-obvious, record it via 'wolfcastle knowledge add', future agents read it in their context. Mention the token budget and pruning mechanism.

2. docs/humans/configuration.md: Document the knowledge config section. Explain that knowledge.max_tokens (default 2000) controls how large the knowledge file can grow before requiring pruning.

3. docs/humans/config-reference.md: Add the knowledge.max_tokens field to the reference table with its type, default, and description.

Match the tone and depth of existing documentation in each file.
acceptance_criteria: All three files contain accurate knowledge file documentation

tasks.title: Add CLI documentation pages for knowledge commands
tasks.description: Add CLI documentation pages for knowledge commands
tasks.body:
Update docs/humans/cli.md (or add new pages under docs/humans/cli/ if that directory structure exists) to document the four knowledge commands:

1. wolfcastle knowledge add "<entry>" - append a knowledge entry to the current namespace's file. Document the budget enforcement behavior and error message.
2. wolfcastle knowledge show - display the current namespace's knowledge file
3. wolfcastle knowledge edit - open the knowledge file in $EDITOR
4. wolfcastle knowledge prune - open the knowledge file for pruning, report token count after

Include usage examples, flags, and typical workflows. Follow the format of existing CLI documentation.
acceptance_criteria: CLI documentation covers all four knowledge commands with examples

tasks.title: Update agent documentation and README
tasks.body:
Update agent-facing documentation and the README to reference codebase knowledge files:

1. AGENTS.md: Explain knowledge files as a context source that agents receive each iteration. Note when agents should update them (non-obvious codebase facts not documented elsewhere). Reference the wolfcastle knowledge add command.

2. docs/agents/ files: Update relevant agent guides (architecture.md, daemon.md, or others as appropriate) to mention knowledge files as part of the context pipeline. Explain where knowledge content appears in the iteration context (after class guidance, before AARs).

3. README.md: Mention knowledge files in the context section alongside ADRs, specs, and AARs. Keep it brief; the README should give a high-level overview, not repeat the full docs.
acceptance_criteria: and README all reference knowledge files

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Documentation is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify all documentation references match implemented behavior (command names, flag names, error messages)

breadcrumbs.text: Updated three human docs pages for knowledge files: how-it-works.md (new section on lifecycle, context injection, pruning), configuration.md (knowledge config subsection with JSON example), config-reference.md (knowledge.max_tokens field reference)
Created four CLI doc pages (knowledge-add, knowledge-show, knowledge-edit, knowledge-prune) under docs/humans/cli/ and added Knowledge row to docs/humans/cli.md command table

Updated AGENTS.md (new Codebase Knowledge Files section), docs/agents/daemon.md (new Codebase Knowledge Injection section), and README.md (knowledge files mentioned in Context paragraph) to reference codebase knowledge files

Audit reviewed 10 files across 3 tasks: how-it-works.md (knowledge section), configuration.md (knowledge subsection), config-reference.md (knowledge.max_tokens), 4 CLI doc pages (knowledge-add/show/edit/prune), cli.md (Knowledge row), AGENTS.md (knowledge section), docs/agents/daemon.md (knowledge injection section), README.md (Context paragraph). PASS. All documentation claims verified against implementation in cmd/knowledge/, internal/knowledge/, and internal/pipeline/context_builder.go. Token budget default (2000), vi fallback, --json non-interactive mode, budget checking before write, and context injection order (after class guidance, before AARs) all confirmed. Cross-references between docs are consistent. One pre-existing issue noted: gofmt violations in 3 test files not touched by this node. Quality: clean, well-integrated documentation that follows existing tone and structure throughout.

audit.result_summary: Documented the codebase knowledge files feature across all human-facing and agent-facing documentation. Three tasks updated 10 files total: how-it-works.md gained a Codebase Knowledge Files section covering lifecycle, context injection, and pruning; configuration.md gained a Codebase Knowledge subsection with JSON example and budget explanation; config-reference.md gained the knowledge.max_tokens field reference; four CLI doc pages (knowledge-add, knowledge-show, knowledge-edit, knowledge-prune) were created following the established format; cli.md was updated with a Knowledge row; AGENTS.md gained a knowledge section with CLI examples; docs/agents/daemon.md gained a Codebase Knowledge Injection section describing context ordering; and README.md now mentions knowledge files in the Context paragraph. All documentation verified against the implementation during audit. No gaps found.

codebase-knowledge-files/documentation/a.objective: Verify all documentation work in the codebase-knowledge-files/documentation node is complete and correct
codebase-knowledge-files/documentation/a.what_happened: Reviewed 10 files across 3 tasks. Verified every documentation claim against the actual implementation in cmd/knowledge/, internal/knowledge/, and internal/pipeline/context_builder.go. Build passes, all tests pass. gofmt issues exist in 3 files but none were introduced by this node. All cross-references between doc pages are consistent. No gaps found.
went_well: All three tasks produced documentation that accurately reflects the implementation
Cross-references between docs pages (how-it-works, configuration, config-reference, CLI pages, AGENTS.md, daemon.md, README.md) form a coherent web with no dead links
Context injection order claim (after class guidance, before AARs) confirmed against context_builder.go line 144-153
improvements: None identified; the documentation is accurate and well-structured
action_items: Fix pre-existing gofmt violations in internal/daemon/coverage_test.go, internal/daemon/iteration_test.go, and internal/knowledge/knowledge_test.go (escalated)

codebase-knowledge-files/documentation/t.objective: Add CLI documentation pages for the four knowledge commands
codebase-knowledge-files/documentation/t.what_happened: Created four new CLI doc pages under docs/humans/cli/: knowledge-add.md, knowledge-show.md, knowledge-edit.md, knowledge-prune.md. Each follows the established format (What It Does, Usage, Flags, Arguments, Exit Codes, Consequences, See Also). Updated docs/humans/cli.md to add a Knowledge row to the commands table linking all four pages. Details were sourced from the actual command implementations in cmd/knowledge/ to ensure accuracy (budget enforcement behavior, --json semantics, editor fallback to vi, prune's interactive vs non-interactive modes).
went_well: All four pages match the tone and structure of existing CLI docs (adr-create, inbox-add, config-set used as reference)
The prune page includes a Typical Workflow section showing the add-reject-prune-retry cycle, which is the most common user scenario
improvements: Exit codes are reported as 0/1 since the cobra commands return generic errors; if exit codes get more specific later the docs will need updating
action_items: AGENTS.md and docs/agents/ updates for knowledge files still pending as separate tasks

task-0001.objective: Update human-facing documentation to cover codebase knowledge files across three docs pages
task-0001.what_happened: Added a Codebase Knowledge Files section to how-it-works.md explaining the lifecycle (discover, record via CLI, future agents read it), the file location, what makes a good entry, and the token budget/pruning mechanism. Added a Codebase Knowledge subsection to configuration.md under Common Customizations showing the JSON config and explaining the budget tradeoff. Added a knowledge section to config-reference.md with the knowledge.max_tokens field documented in the same format as all other fields.
went_well: All three files follow the existing tone and structure of their surroundings
The how-it-works section ties knowledge files to the existing artifact taxonomy (ADRs, specs, AARs) without redundancy
improvements: The config-reference section only documents max_tokens since that is the only field in the spec; if more fields are added later, the section will need expansion
action_items: CLI reference pages for wolfcastle knowledge add/show/prune still need to be created under docs/humans/cli/

task-0003.objective: Update AGENTS.md, agent docs, and README to reference codebase knowledge files
task-0003.what_happened: Added a Codebase Knowledge Files section to AGENTS.md explaining what the files are, when agents should update them, and the wolfcastle knowledge add command. Added a Codebase Knowledge Injection section to docs/agents/daemon.md describing where knowledge content appears in the context pipeline (after class guidance, before AARs), that it's read fresh each iteration, and what kind of entries belong there. Updated the Context paragraph in README.md to mention codebase knowledge files alongside ADRs, specs, and AARs with a link to the how-it-works docs.
went_well: Each addition matches the tone and structure of its surrounding content
The AGENTS.md section includes a concrete CLI example which makes it immediately actionable
improvements: The README addition makes the Context paragraph longer; if the section grows further it may warrant splitting into sub-bullets
action_items: None; this completes the documentation pass for codebase knowledge files
